text: This work intends to provide an interpretative model to assess the competitiveness of tourism destinations that are rich in tangible or intangible cultural assets and which refer to local development strategies that include the cultural and creative industry sector. Starting from a thorough analysis of international and national literature on the creative industries and their impact on regional development within cultural heritage contexts, this research proposes a management model that is instrumental to developing the integrated policies that can support the creative tourism destination (CTD). Even though destination management is a consolidated discipline in scientific research, there is still a clear gap in relation to the synergies between cultural heritage, creative industry sector and local development, a factor that, while not innovating by itself, contributes significantly to opening up a new theoretical frontier on a scientific level. In any case, the research outcomes intend to bring an added value to the ongoing debate on the subject matter, providing valuable tools for the support of regional development and tourism policy.
